proofs.proof_tex:
Fix any integer $n\ge 1$. By $0<\alpha<1$ in ass:linear-center, $b=1-\alpha^2>0$. At $\delta=0$, $f_0(z)=\alpha z$, so def:mirror-anm-paths gives $P_{+,0}=\operatorname{Law}(Z,\alpha Z+E)=P_0$. The density of this law is
\[p_{+,0}(x,y)= rac{1}{2\pi\sqrt b}\exp\left\{- rac{x^2}{2}- rac{(y-\alpha x)^2}{2b}ight\}.\]
The reverse construction gives $P_{-,0}=\operatorname{Law}(\alpha Z+E,Z)$, whose density is
\[p_{-,0}(x,y)= rac{1}{2\pi\sqrt b}\exp\left\{- rac{y^2}{2}- rac{(x-\alpha y)^2}{2b}ight\}.\]
Since $b=1-\alpha^2$, direct expansion yields
\[b x^2+(y-\alpha x)^2=x^2+y^2-2\alpha xy=b y^2+(x-\alpha y)^2.\]
Consequently $p_{+,0}(x,y)=p_{-,0}(x,y)$ for every $(x,y)$, and hence $P_{+,0}=P_{-,0}=P_0$.

It remains to evaluate the minimax risk in def:chernoff-experiment. For any $\pi\in T_n$, put $q=P_0^n\{\pi=+\}$. Because the two hypotheses induce the same sample law,
\[P_{+,0}^n\{\pi
e +\}=1-q,\qquad P_{-,0}^n\{\pi
e -\}=q.\]
Thus $\max\{1-q,q\}\ge 1/2$, and taking the infimum over $\pi$ shows $R_n(0)\ge 1/2$. For the reverse inequality, define the Borel rule $\pi^*((x_i,y_i)_{i=1}^n)=+$ when $x_1\ge0$ and $\pi^*=-$ when $x_1<0$. Under $P_0^n$, $X_1=Z_1\sim N(0,1)$, so $P_0^n\{\pi^*=+\}=P_0^n\{\pi^*=-\}=1/2$. Therefore both error probabilities of $\pi^*$ equal $1/2$, giving $R_n(0)\le1/2$. Combining the bounds proves $R_n(0)=1/2$ for every $n$.

open_obligations.what_is_open: Primitive, verifiable conditions on a concrete two-fold nuisance-training procedure that imply log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)) along every stated admissible sequence.
open_obligations.obstruction: The maintained training-random L4 O_P(r_n) premise controls typical nuisance error but does not control exponentially tilted expectations on vanishing-probability training events. The rare-event perturbation in def:rare-event-nuisance-perturbation can have arbitrarily large amplitude on events whose probability tends to zero and therefore remain compatible with an O_P rate, while such events need not be negligible on the n C_star(delta_n) logarithmic scale.
open_obligations.attempted: Conditioning on the returned-function sigma-field G_n and applying the L4 variance comparison controls ordinary-probability consistency, but it supplies neither an exponential moment bound nor a deterministic envelope for the returned functions. The deterministic q_n=1/log(n+e) perturbation on the faster subsequence n C_star(delta_n)/log log(n+e)->infinity diagnoses this missing tail control but does not identify a concrete learner satisfying the desired tilted bound.
open_obligations.partial_result: Direct change of measure from the definitions gives P_{+,delta_n}^n{widehat Delta_n<0}=A_n^n K_{+,n} and P_{-,delta_n}^n{widehat Delta_n>=0}=A_n^n K_{-,n}. Thus the requested o(n C_star) control of both log K terms is exactly sufficient for the score-contrast rule to inherit the Chernoff exponent; the frozen L4-in-probability premise alone certifies only the weaker consistency conclusion in prop:score-contrast-template. The OEQ remains unresolved.
